calibration.historicalIssuanceNote: These intervals are migration diagnostics and were not part of the historically issued point forecasts.
calibration.method: Rolling absolute-residual coverage envelopes using only previously resolved errors.

exclusions: Rows missing a finite actual, model prediction, naïve prediction, or timestamp.

Legacy forecast rows whose stored point cannot be matched exactly to the immediately preceding history record are excluded from governed forecast-record migration but retained in aggregate evaluation.
limitations: The frozen period is short and operational rather than a randomized sample.
Three legacy rows are not eligible for governed record migration because their points do not match the immediately preceding retained history row.
The model MAE is higher than the naive MAE in this frozen sample; no performance claim is supported.
Migration intervals test calibration mechanics but were not issued with the historical point forecasts.

excludedLegacyRows.reason: Stored evaluation points do not exactly match the immediately preceding history row; the legacy result is retained for aggregate evaluation but is not rewritten as a governed forecast.
Stored evaluation points do not exactly match the immediately preceding history row; the legacy result is retained for aggregate evaluation but is not rewritten as a governed forecast.
Stored evaluation points do not exactly match the immediately preceding history row; the legacy result is retained for aggregate evaluation but is not rewritten as a governed forecast.

missingDataHandling: No imputation and no timestamp interpolation.

rollingOrigin.referenceBaselineWarmStart: First expanding-mean prediction uses the recorded naive point.

sourceResolutionRule: Resolve each stored one-step point forecast against the next actual value recorded in data/forecast_eval.jsonl.
